results.classification_rationale: At least 2 confirmed de novo unrelated cases with CPVT1 documented: one published case (Ohno2015, PMID 26114861, confirmed de novo by trio) and a clinical proband confirmed de novo by trio analysis (ClinVar VCGS SCV003921799). At least one additional CPVT1 case is cited from other literature (PMIDs 23595086, 29434162, 29453246) per VCGS comment. Variant absent from gnomAD, located in RYR2 C-terminal hot-spot domain, classified P/LP by 3 independent ClinVar labs. Meets ACMG Pathogenic threshold of ≥2 confirmed de novo unrelated cases.
results.description: This variant has strong previous evidence of pathogenicity in unrelated individuals. At least 2 confirmed de novo unrelated cases with catecholaminergic polymorphic ventricular tachycardia type 1 (CPVT1) are documented: one published male patient (onset age 10 with syncope, confirmed de novo by trio; PMID 26114861) and a clinical proband confirmed de novo by trio analysis (ClinVar SCV003921799, VCGS). At least one additional CPVT1 case is cited from other literature (PMIDs 23595086, 29434162, 29453246). The variant is absent from gnomAD (v2/v3/v4), located in the RYR2 C-terminal hot-spot region, predicted damaging by multiple in silico tools, and classified as Pathogenic or Likely Pathogenic by 3 independent laboratory submitters in ClinVar.
results.notes:
**Summary:** Pathogenic. ClinVar classifies this variant as P/LP (2 stars, 3 independent laboratory submissions: 1P + 2LP). Evidence meets ACMG Pathogenic criteria based on ≥2 confirmed de novo unrelated cases with CPVT1. Key evidence: (1) [Patient 31 — published confirmed de novo case from Ohno2015](#cite:Ohno2015 "31 | M | 28 | 10 | Syncope | 14174a>g | Y4725C | CT | Trio | de novo | none | none") (male, onset age 10 with syncope); (2) a second confirmed de novo proband from VCGS clinical testing (ClinVar SCV003921799, trio analysis); (3) at least one additional CPVT1 case from PMIDs 23595086, 29434162, 29453246 cited by VCGS. Variant is absent from gnomAD (v2/v3/v4), in silico predictions unanimously damaging, and located in the RYR2 C-terminal hot-spot domain. No refuting evidence identified.

**Supporting evidence:**
- **Ohno2015** (PMID 26114861): [Patient 31 — male, age 28 at analysis, onset age 10 with syncope, RYR2 p.Y4725C confirmed de novo by trio, both parents phenotypically unaffected](#cite:Ohno2015 "31 | M | 28 | 10 | Syncope | 14174a>g | Y4725C | CT | Trio | de novo | none | none"). Primary published de novo case for this variant.
- **Ohno2021** (PMID 33606749, correction to Ohno2015): [Correction confirms Patient 31 data identical](#cite:Ohno2021 "31 | M | 28 | 10 | syncope | 14174a>g | Y4725C | CT | Trio | de novo | none | none") — same individual as Ohno2015, counted once only; de novo status unchanged.
- **ClinVar (VCGS, SCV003921799, Pathogenic)**: New confirmed de novo CPVT1 proband (parental status confirmed, trio analysis). Applied criteria: absent gnomAD SP; multiple in silico tools damaging SP; alternative missense changes at same residue (Y4725N, Y4725H) classified LP in ClinVar/LOVD SP; ≥2 published individuals with CPVT1 (PMIDs 23595086, 26114861, 29434162, 29453246) SP; confirmed de novo in proband SP. CPVT1 penetrance estimated 60–70%.
- **ClinVar (GeneDx, SCV005201507, LP)**: Absent from gnomAD; in silico damaging; located in one of three RYR2 hot-spot regions per PMID 19926015. Cites additional PMIDs 32152366, 34697415, 35135837, 37291213.
- **ClinVar (Labcorp/Invitae, SCV001577112, LP)**: LP classification with criteria provided.

**Deduplication note:** Ohno2015 (PMID 26114861) and Ohno2021 (PMID 33606749) describe the same patient (Patient 31) — counted as one case. Deduplicated total: ≥3 unrelated cases (1 confirmed de novo from Ohno2015; ≥1 additional CPVT1 case from PMIDs 23595086/29434162/29453246 per VCGS; 1 VCGS clinical proband confirmed de novo). At least 2 confirmed de novo cases. ACMG Pathogenic threshold met.

papers.rank_rationale: Original published report providing the primary confirmed de novo case (Patient 31, CPVT onset age 10, syncope) with trio-verified de novo status, the most load-bearing evidence for the Pathogenic classification.
Correction to Ohno2015 confirming identical de novo case data for Patient 31; ranked second as it corroborates but contributes no new independent case.

claims.text: Patient 31 (male, age 28 at genetic analysis, onset age 10, presenting with syncope) carries RYR2 c.14174a>g (p.Y4725C) in the C-terminal domain, confirmed de novo by trio testing with both parents phenotypically unaffected.

citations.quote: 31 | M | 28 | 10 | Syncope | 14174a>g | Y4725C | CT | Trio | de novo | none | none

claims.text: Patient 31 (male, age 28 at genetic analysis, onset age 10, most severe symptom syncope) carries RYR2 c.14174a>g (p.Y4725C) located in the C-terminal domain, confirmed de novo by trio testing, with both parents phenotypically unaffected — correction paper confirming the same individual as reported in Ohno2015.
